root_cause.text: Keycloak lacked the ability to extract and map user identities from the `otherName` field of the Subject Alternative Name (SAN) extension in X.509 client certificates, which prevented authentication for certificates relying on User Principal Names (UPN) such as those used for Microsoft Smart Card Login.
root_cause.evidence: The patch introduces `MAPPING_SOURCE_CERT_SUBJECTALTNAME_OTHERNAME` and modifies `UserIdentityExtractor` to parse the `otherName` (generalName 0) structure, specifically looking for the `UPN_OID` ("1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.3") to extract the user identity.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: Keycloak lacked the ability to extract and map user identities from the `otherName` field of the Subject Alternative Name (SAN) extension in X.509 client certificates, which prevented authentication for certificates relying on User Principal Names (UPN) such as those used for Microsoft Smart Card Login.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch introduces `MAPPING_SOURCE_CERT_SUBJECTALTNAME_OTHERNAME` and modifies `UserIdentityExtractor` to parse the `otherName` (generalName 0) structure, specifically looking for the `UPN_OID` ("1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.3") to extract the user identity.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a constant for mapping UPN from Subject Alternative Name.
intent_record.what: Added a new string constant MAPPING_SOURCE_CERT_SUBJECTALTNAME_OTHERNAME.
intent_record.how: Provides a new mapping source identifier for extracting UPN from a certificate's Subject Alternative Name.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add support for extracting user identity from the SubjectAltName otherName field.
intent_record.what: Added a new switch case for SUBJECTALTNAME_OTHERNAME which sets the extractor using index 0.
intent_record.how: Enables the system to process certificates by extracting the user identity from the 'otherName' field of the Subject Alternative Name.

intent_record.intent_summary: Import the MAPPING_SOURCE_CERT_SUBJECTALTNAME_OTHERNAME constant to support new X509 certificate mapping features.
intent_record.what: Added a static import for the MAPPING_SOURCE_CERT_SUBJECTALTNAME_OTHERNAME constant.
intent_record.how: Allows the class to reference the new constant directly, facilitating mapping from the certificate's Subject Alternative Name 'otherName' field.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add support for mapping users via the Subject Alternative Name OtherName field in X.509 certificates.
intent_record.what: Added MAPPING_SOURCE_CERT_SUBJECTALTNAME_OTHERNAME to the existing list of mapping sources.
intent_record.how: Allows the authenticator to extract and use the OtherName field from the Subject Alternative Name extension of an X.509 certificate.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add imports for ASN.1 parsing utilities.
intent_record.what: Added imports for BouncyCastle ASN.1 classes and ByteArrayInputStream.
intent_record.how: Provides the necessary classes to process ASN.1 structures and byte streams in the subsequent code.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add UPN OID constant for User Principal Name extraction.
intent_record.what: Adds a static final string constant representing the UPN OID.
intent_record.how: Provides a predefined OID string constant for extracting User Principal Names from certificates.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix extraction of identities from 'otherName' Subject Alternative Names by implementing ASN.1 parsing for UPN OIDs.
intent_record.what: Replaced a simple iteration over subject alternative names with a detailed ASN.1 parsing mechanism for 'otherName' types and added an unwrap helper method.
intent_record.how: Correctly decodes byte arrays of 'otherName' subject alternative names to extract specific OIDs like UPN, preventing raw byte objects from being returned incorrectly.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add SUBJECTALTNAME_OTHERNAME to certificate mapping sources.
intent_record.what: Added SUBJECTALTNAME_OTHERNAME to the enumeration.
intent_record.how: Provides an additional option for mapping certificate data.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add unit tests for the Subject Alternative Name (SAN) identity extractor.
intent_record.what: A new unit test class, SubjectAltNameIdentityExtractorTest, is added.
intent_record.how: It introduces tests to verify that the UserIdentityExtractor correctly extracts the otherName (UPN) and email fields from an X509 certificate.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a PEM-encoded X.509 test certificate for testing purposes.
intent_record.what: Added a new file containing a PEM-encoded X.509 certificate.
intent_record.how: Provides a test certificate that can be used by the test suite to verify certificate parsing or authentication logic.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove stale or unnecessary test-user certificate.
intent_record.what: Removed a PEM-encoded X.509 certificate file.
intent_record.how: Eliminates the certificate from the source tree, preventing it from being used or distributed.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a PEM-encoded X.509 client certificate for testing purposes.
intent_record.what: A new PEM-encoded X.509 certificate file was added.
intent_record.how: Provides a certificate payload that can be loaded during execution, likely for testing authentication, TLS, or signature verification.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update default certificate configuration values for local testing.
intent_record.what: Changed default email address and added a default common name in a certificate configuration file.
intent_record.how: Updates the default values used for generating test certificates to use 'test-user' and 'test-user@localhost'.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update OpenSSL configuration to use hardcoded Subject Alternative Names for testing client certificates.
intent_record.what: Replaced dynamic subjectAltName directives with a static section defining explicit email, IP, DNS, URI, and otherName values, and commented out the authorityKeyIdentifier directive.
intent_record.how: Modifies the certificate generation process to embed specific, hardcoded Subject Alternative Names rather than deriving them dynamically from the certificate request.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update numeric value from 1001 to 1007.
intent_record.what: Changed the numeric value from 1001 to 1007.
intent_record.how: Updates a hardcoded identifier or configuration value to a new number.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix certificate filename in resource inclusion list.
intent_record.what: Changed a certificate filename in the resource includes list from test-user-san-email@localhost.cert.pem to test-user-san@localhost.cert.pem.
intent_record.how: Ensures the build system packages the correct certificate file by referencing its updated name.

intent_record.intent_summary: Include client certificates in the build resources.
intent_record.what: Added a new resource directory mapping for client certificates in the build configuration.
intent_record.how: Instructs the build system to include client certificate files from the specified PKI directory into the compiled artifact.

intent_record.intent_summary: Expand file include patterns to ensure additional certificate and key files are copied during the build process.
intent_record.what: Added *.crl, *.key, and certs/clients/* to the <includes> configuration element in a Maven plugin execution.
intent_record.how: Ensures that certificate revocation lists, key files, and client certificates are included alongside keystores and truststores when preparing the auth-server-undertow container directory.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove unused PhantomJSBrowser import.
intent_record.what: Removed the import statement for PhantomJSBrowser.
intent_record.how: Cleans up the file by removing an unused import without affecting runtime behavior.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add static import for SUBJECTALTNAME_OTHERNAME.
intent_record.what: Added a static import for the SUBJECTALTNAME_OTHERNAME mapping source type.
intent_record.how: Makes the SUBJECTALTNAME_OTHERNAME constant available for unqualified use within the class.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor PhantomJS mutual TLS configuration into a reusable method.
intent_record.what: Extracted the PhantomJS mutual TLS configuration logic from onBeforeTestClass into a new parameterized configurePhantomJS method.
intent_record.how: Allows different tests or subclasses to reuse the PhantomJS configuration logic with custom certificate and key file paths, without changing the default execution behavior.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add alternative email and UPN attributes to the test user for expanded X509 authentication testing.
intent_record.what: Added 'alternative_email' and 'upn' attributes to the user object during test setup.
intent_record.how: Provides the test user with additional identity attributes, enabling test cases that verify X509 authentication against these specific fields.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update and expand test configuration methods for X509 authentication mapping to user attributes.
intent_record.what: Renamed and modified an existing test configuration method and added a new one for X509 authenticator configs.
intent_record.how: Updates the test data generation to use USER_ATTRIBUTE mapping types with specific custom attribute names instead of USERNAME_EMAIL.

intent_record.intent_summary: Rename the X509 test class to remove the specific 'Email' designation.
intent_record.what: The test class name was changed from X509BrowserLoginSubjectAltNameEmailTest to X509BrowserLoginSubjectAltNameTest.
intent_record.how: This updates the class identifier to reflect a broader or more accurate scope without altering the underlying test execution logic.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor PhantomJS CLI configuration logic into a reusable helper method.
intent_record.what: Replaced inline logic for building PhantomJS CLI arguments with a call to a new configurePhantomJS helper method.
intent_record.how: Encapsulates the PhantomJS configuration into a single method call, maintaining the same configuration parameters while reducing code duplication.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix test assertion to correctly expect the email address in the distinguished name.
intent_record.what: Updated test assertion from assertTrue with equals to assertEquals and modified the expected string.
intent_record.how: Modifies the test to expect an EMAILADDRESS component in the subject distinguished name and improves assertion failure output.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update SAN Email test and add new test for SAN UPN.
intent_record.what: Renamed a test method, updated its configuration and expected parameters, and added a new test method for SAN UPN.
intent_record.how: Modifies the test suite to properly verify login functionality using Subject Alternative Name (SAN) Email and UPN mapped to user attributes.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add SSL and XNIO imports to support enhanced secure connection configuration.
intent_record.what: Added imports for org.xnio.Options, org.xnio.SslClientAuthMode, KeyManager, TrustManager, and TrustManagerFactory.
intent_record.how: Makes SSL and XNIO related classes available for use in the file to configure secure connections.

intent_record.intent_summary: Configure the HTTPS listener to request SSL client certificates.
intent_record.what: Added a socket option to the Undertow builder to set the SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_MODE to REQUESTED.
intent_record.how: Configures the HTTPS listener to request client certificates during the TLS handshake, allowing but not requiring mutual TLS.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor SSL context creation to support loading both custom key managers and trust managers.
intent_record.what: Extracted key manager loading into a separate method and updated SSLContext initialization to include trust managers.
intent_record.how: Enables the server to configure custom trust managers alongside key managers for TLS connections, rather than relying solely on the default SSL context when a keystore is provided.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor TLS configuration to separate KeyManager creation and add support for custom truststores.
intent_record.what: Refactored the TLS configuration to return KeyManagers and added a new getTrustManagers method to load a truststore.
intent_record.how: Separates the creation of KeyManagers and TrustManagers into distinct methods, enabling custom truststore loading from a system property.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add default configurations for SAML ports and x509 certificate lookup providers.
intent_record.what: Added JSON configuration blocks for 'login-protocol' and 'x509cert-lookup'.
intent_record.how: Enables SAML protocol port mappings and configures x509 certificate extraction for various proxy servers like HAProxy, Apache, and Nginx.

grouping_result.intent: Update SAN Email test and add new test for SAN UPN.
Add imports for ASN.1 parsing utilities.
Fix extraction of identities from 'otherName' Subject Alternative Names by implementing ASN.1 parsing for UPN OIDs.
Add unit tests for the Subject Alternative Name (SAN) identity extractor.
Update OpenSSL configuration to use hardcoded Subject Alternative Names for testing client certificates.
Remove stale or unnecessary test-user certificate.
Fix certificate filename in resource inclusion list.
Expand file include patterns to ensure additional certificate and key files are copied during the build process.
Refactor PhantomJS CLI configuration logic into a reusable helper method.
Rename the X509 test class to remove the specific 'Email' designation.
Fix test assertion to correctly expect the email address in the distinguished name.
Configure the HTTPS listener to request SSL client certificates.
Refactor SSL context creation to support loading both custom key managers and trust managers.
Refactor TLS configuration to separate KeyManager creation and add support for custom truststores.
Add default configurations for SAML ports and x509 certificate lookup providers.

consistency_review.core_intent: Add support and tests for extracting and mapping user identity from X509 Subject Alternative Name (SAN) OtherName and UPN fields.
Add and configure test certificates and OpenSSL settings for local testing.
Expand file include patterns to copy additional certificate and key files during the build process.
Refactor PhantomJS configuration logic into reusable helper methods.
Configure the HTTPS listener with SSL support and client certificates.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly implements the missing functionality to extract and map user identities from the otherName field and UPN in X.509 certificates, which is the exact root cause of the vulnerability.
Adding imports for ASN.1 parsing utilities is a necessary supporting change for extracting the 'otherName' field from X.509 certificate extensions.
The group's intent to fix the extraction of identities from the 'otherName' Subject Alternative Names and implement ASN.1 parsing for UPN OIDs directly addresses the root cause of the vulnerability.
The group adds unit tests for the Subject Alternative Name (SAN) identity extractor, which directly tests the newly added functionality that addresses the vulnerability root cause.

The group adds test certificates and configures OpenSSL settings to include Subject Alternative Names, which directly supports testing the fix for the vulnerability involving X.509 client certificates and the 'otherName' SAN field.
Removing a stale or unnecessary test-user certificate is a cleanup action and does not directly address the vulnerability of lacking identity extraction from the otherName field.
Updating a numeric value is an unrelated change that does not address the logic for extracting user identities from the X.509 SAN otherName field.

The group only contains a minor fix to a certificate filename in a resource inclusion list, which does not address the core vulnerability of extracting and mapping user identities from the otherName field of the SAN extension.
The group only modifies build configurations to include test certificates, which is an ancillary change for testing rather than directly addressing the vulnerability root cause.
The group removes an unused PhantomJSBrowser import, which is a minor cleanup entirely unrelated to the X.509 SAN otherName extraction vulnerability.

The addition of the static import for SUBJECTALTNAME_OTHERNAME directly supports the implementation of extracting identities from the otherName field of the SAN extension, which is the core of the vulnerability fix.
The group only contains refactoring of PhantomJS configuration logic into reusable helper methods, which has no direct relation to the X.509 client certificate SAN parsing vulnerability.
The group only renames a test class, which is a refactoring change and does not directly address the root cause of the vulnerability.

The group only contains a fix for a test assertion regarding an email address in a distinguished name, which is unrelated to the core vulnerability of adding support for extracting User Principal Names from the otherName field of the SAN extension.

The group consists of generic test infrastructure configuration to enable SSL and request client certificates, rather than addressing the specific logic for extracting user identities from the `otherName` field in the SAN extension.
The group's intent to refactor SSL context creation is completely unrelated to extracting user identities from the 'otherName' field of the SAN extension in X.509 client certificates.
The group focuses on refactoring TLS configuration and KeyManager creation, which is unrelated to parsing X.509 client certificate SAN extensions or fixing the UPN identity extraction issue.

The group only adds default configurations for SAML ports and x509 certificate lookup providers, which is not directly related to extracting or mapping user identities from the otherName field of the SAN extension.

candidates.rationale: This line returns the raw value from the Subject Alternative Name without ASN.1 parsing, which is required to extract UPNs from the 'otherName' field.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement directly returns the unparsed value of the Subject Alternative Name, failing to properly parse ASN.1 structures required to extract UPNs from the 'otherName' field.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: [KEYCLOAK-6116] - Get email attribute from 'subject alternative name' using X509 certificate
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 0a4fd79b225df03d769a267c9b464893a36753c6
Author: pedroigor <pigor.craveiro@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Feb 16 16:14:32 2018 -0200

    [KEYCLOAK-6116] - Get email attribute from 'subject alternative name' using X509 certificate

diff --git a/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/authenticators/x509/UserIdentityExtractor.java b/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/authenticators/x509/UserIdentityExtractor.java
index ef29aec5b1..881fdb7671 100644
--- a/services/src/mai
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces `SubjectAltNameExtractor` to extract identities from the SAN extension, but it simply returns `next.get(1)`. For `otherName` (general name 0), this returns a DER-encoded byte array rather than parsing the User Principal Name (UPN) string, leaving the root cause unaddressed.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
KEYCLOAK-4335: x509 client certificate authentication

Started on implementing cert thumbprint validation as a part of x
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit b2f10359c8c33dd0a843c3ee28e0c8ecf275e834
Author: Peter Nalyvayko <brat000012001@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Jul 26 11:47:28 2016 -0400

    KEYCLOAK-4335: x509 client certificate authentication
    
    Started on implementing cert thumbprint validation as a part of x509 auth flow. Added a prompt screen to give users a choice to either log in based on the identity extracted from X509 cert or to continue with normal browser login flow authentication; clean up some of the comments
    
    x509
bic_trace_step.reason: The newly introduced UserIdentityExtractor class implements extractors like X500NameRDNExtractor and PatternMatcher, but entirely lacks an implementation for extracting user identities from the Subject Alternative Name (SAN) extension or its otherName field. Therefore, the vulnerability (lack of SAN/UPN extraction capability) is present.
